warning: A recursive definition without a base case can fail to terminate.
A recursive step that does not reduce problem size may loop indefinitely.
Some recursive algorithms repeat subproblems unnecessarily.
mitigation: Use memoization or tabulation when overlap exists.

warning: Recursive graph traversal can revisit cycles indefinitely.
Recursive updates can amplify error, bias, or instability.
mitigation: Validate against external data and monitor feedback.

warning: Recursive generation can continue beyond meaningful grounding.
mitigation: Use stopping conditions, verification, and review.

warning: An incorrect base case invalidates an induction proof.
mitigation: Check base cases and boundary values.
